subject: [PATCH v2 5/9] nfsd: widen nfsd_genl_rqstp address fields to sockaddr_storage

2026-06-02.patch_summary: This patch fixes an issue in the nfsd kernel module where IPv6 addresses were being truncated when sent to userspace via netlink attributes. The problem occurred because the address fields in the struct nfsd_genl_rqstp were declared as plain 'struct sockaddr' (16 bytes), which is not large enough to hold an IPv6 address. To fix this, the patch widens these fields to 'struct sockaddr_storage', which can hold larger addresses. Additionally, it ensures that the full address is captured in memcpy calls and initializes the genl_rqstp stack variable to prevent leaking uninitialized data. This patch resolves a bug introduced by a previous commit and fixes an issue that could be exploited by unprivileged processes.
